signals.headline: XRP Records Largest Weekly Inflows, Unseating Bitcoin and Solana Amid U
signals.raw:
XRP Records Largest Weekly Inflows, Unseating Bitcoin and Solana Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Rally | YES | HIGH confidence**  
XRP led digital asset inflows with $119.6 million last week—its strongest since mid-December 2025—surpassing Bitcoin's $107.3 million and Solana's $34.9 million, boosting year-to-date XRP inflows to 7% of assets under management.[1]

signals.headline: Bitcoin April net outflows** stand at $145 million despite weekly gains, tying into prediction markets showing mixed sen
signals.raw:
Bitcoin April net outflows** stand at $145 million despite weekly gains, tying into prediction markets showing mixed sentiment (e.g., Polymarket odds for CLARITY Act at 72%, potentially unlocking $4-8B XRP ETF capital late April).[3]  
Solana shows bullish Polymarket forecasts (87% chance above $80 this April), with strong ecosystem activity.[2]

signals.headline: US and Iran Plan Fresh Talks Before April 7 Ceasefire Expires Next Week | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US and Iran Plan Fresh Talks Before April 7 Ceasefire Expires Next Week | NO | HIGH confidence**  
The US and Iran are discussing a second round of negotiations in Islamabad before the two-week ceasefire—announced April 7—expires early next week, following inconclusive weekend talks, but no deal or extension has been confirmed.[1]

signals.headline: US Begins Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports and Strait of Hormuz | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Begins Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports and Strait of Hormuz | NO | HIGH confidence**  
President Trump has imposed a blockade on Iranian shipping, declaring any Iranian fast attack ships approaching US vessels will be eliminated; Tehran vows retaliation amid fragile ceasefire holding with no direct US Navy attacks reported.[2][3]

signals.headline: South Korea Annual Inflation Holds at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Annual Inflation Holds at 2.0% in February 2026, Unchanged from January and Below Forecasts | NO | LOW confidence**

South Korea's annual inflation rate remained steady at **2.0%** in February 2026, matching the Bank of Korea's target and the prior month's figure, while slightly missing market expectations of 2.1%[2]. This follows January's 2.0% reading, a five-month low that also hit
